news.title: Why The Fed Could Shrink Its Balance Sheet Again (And Markets Might Not Notice)
news.desc: Late last year, the Federal Reserve ended its latest quantitative tightening (QT) program, reducing the balance sheet by more than $2 trillion from a peak of almost $9 trillion. The Fed appears to be implementing policies that seek to reduce banks' demand for reserves, which could allow for gradual balance sheet expansion even without QE programs.

news.title: Fed Balance Sheet & Net Liquidity - Latest Data
news.desc: As of April 8, 2026, the Federal Reserve balance sheet stands at $4,407B. On the liability side, the Treasury General Account (TGA) is at $748B. Data updated April 17, 2026 with latest market closing values showing 10-Yr Treasury at 4.25% and U.S. Dollar at 98.22.

news.title: 流动性环境 - Liquidity Environment Analysis
news.desc: 商业银行存放在美联储的准备金余额是银行进行借贷和交易的基础。美联储认为准备金在 3 万亿美元以上属于'充裕'水平，低于该水平可能引发流动性紧张。数据来源 FRED，图表来自 TradingView。
